topic: Augment Code Review 2026: Enterprise AI Coding Agent with Context Engine

competitors.title: Augment Code Review 2026: Pricing, Features & Ratings
key_points: Rating 4.2/5; pricing from $20/mo (Indie) to $200/mo (Max) plus custom Enterprise
Context Engine processes 400,000+ files simultaneously, building semantic dependency graphs
SWE-bench 70.6% Verified score (vs 54% competitor average)
13x faster than Claude 3.5 Sonnet for code tasks; 10x faster code search than grep
Remote Agents run autonomously in background for multi-step tasks
ISO 42001 and SOC 2 certified — first AI coding platform with ISO 42001
Supported models: proprietary Augment Models, Claude 3.5 Sonnet, GPT-4 Turbo, Code Llama
Limitations: credit-based pricing unpredictable, steep learning curve, not ideal for solo devs or budgets under $60/mo, no offline capability

competitors.title: Augment Code Review (2026) — AICodeReview
key_points: Rating 4.0/5; 20 languages supported, 6 integrations
$252M funding at $977M valuation, backed by Eric Schmidt, Index Ventures, Sutter Hill, Lightspeed, Meritech
Founded 2022 by Igor Ostrovsky and Guy Gur-Ari (former Google AI research scientist)
CEO Scott Dietzen (former Pure Storage CEO), CTO Dion Almaer (former Google engineering director, VP Engineering at Shopify)
~188 employees as of early 2026
SWE-bench Pro 51.80% (#1 among all agents); SWE-bench Verified 70.6% with Claude Sonnet 4
Context Engine indexes 100K+ files with semantic understanding of function signatures, class hierarchies, import chains, API contracts

Community Plan (Free): limited completions/chat, single-repo indexing; Indie $20/mo: 40K credits, ~125 agent messages; Standard $60/mo: 130K pooled credits up to 20 users; Max $200/mo: 450K pooled credits; Enterprise: custom
ISO/IEC 42001 first AI coding assistant with international AI management certification
Competes with GitHub Copilot, Cursor, Sourcegraph Cody, Tabnine
Key differentiator vs Copilot: 70% win rate on complex coding tasks (internal comparison)
Credit consumption varies: 5-20 for autocomplete, 50-200 for function generation, 500-2000 for bug diagnosis, 3000-15000 for architecture refactoring
MCP support launched February 2026 for 100+ native and MCP tools
Feb 2026: Context Engine released as standalone MCP server, claims 70%+ improvement in third-party agent performance

competitors.title: Augment Code vs Claude Code (2026): Context Engine vs Agentic CLI
key_points: Augment SWE-bench Pro 51.8% (#1) vs Claude Code ~34.8% — same Claude Opus 4.5 model, difference is agent architecture
Claude Code SWE-bench Verified 80.9% (record); Augment does not report separately on Verified
Augment: IDE extension + CLI + Context Engine; Claude Code: CLI + VS Code extension + Web
Augment indexes 400K+ files cross-repo with semantic relationships; Claude Code reads files on demand
Context Lineage feature (early 2026): indexes commit history including messages, authors, timestamps, changed files
Feb 2026: Context Engine released as standalone MCP server — plug into Cursor, Claude Code, Zed
Augment supports VS Code, JetBrains; Claude Code supports Terminal, VS Code, Web
Augment: SOC 2 Type II, ISO 42001, CMEK encryption, SIEM; Claude Code: Enterprise plan available
Pricing comparison: Augment Indie $20/mo (40K credits) vs Claude Code Pro $20/mo (Sonnet 4.5); both Max tiers at $200/mo
Hybrid setup recommended: Augment Context Engine MCP plugged into Claude Code for best of both worlds

angles: Enterprise Context Engine vs consumer AI coding: Augment's 400K+ file semantic indexing vs context-window-limited Copilot/Cursor
